title: zephyr: evaluate libSCHC as external module
description: Decision follow-up from SCHC package discussion. Do not swap SCHC libraries casually. Evaluate whether libSCHC can be imported as a Zephyr module or otherwise reused for LICHEN's embedded SCHC implementation. Acceptance: check libSCHC license compatibility with GPL-3.0 distribution; measure code size and RAM impact on constrained targets, especially STM32WL; map libSCHC's rule-table model to LICHEN rules and constants; verify LoRa/non-LoRa assumptions and fragmentation behavior; run or prototype against existing LICHEN SCHC test vectors; document any gaps versus current lichen/subsys/lichen/schc behavior before recommending adoption, wrapping, or rejection.
acceptance_criteria: After implementation, run the relevant tests/builds and then complete two separate code-review passes before closing. Each review pass should inspect the produced code against this Bead's acceptance criteria and file follow-up Beads for any discovered issues.
notes: 2026-06-30 evaluation result: do not import/swap libSCHC for embedded Zephyr nodes now. Upstream inspected at imec-idlab/libschc a01d7856243d3aa1242918e31649d8a5ee92872e. License is GPL-3.0 dual/commercial, so GPL path is compatible with LICHEN GPL firmware. Upstream README states the library is experimental and missing some RFC 8724 features. Integration model is not a Zephyr module: plain C sources with compile-time schc_config.h and rules/rule_config.h includes, example rules, and platform callbacks for fragmentation timers/send paths. Rule model is generic RFC-style fields (MO/CDA, layer-specific IPv6/UDP/CoAP rule structs, per-device compression/fragmentation contexts) and could express some LICHEN rules, but LICHEN would need a custom rule table adapter and exact byte-vector conformance work. LoRa fragmentation exists and has NO_ACK/ACK_ON_ERROR/ACK_ALWAYS-style state machinery, but brings connection state, mbuf pools, timer callbacks, bitmap/window configuration, and experimental limitations that need a dedicated fit study before use on STM32WL. Rough arm-zephyr-eabi -Os object footprint for libSCHC example config: about 41.9 KiB text and 3.8 KiB BSS before Zephyr/linker GC. Same rough compiler measurement for current lichen/subsys/lichen/schc/schc.c: 6.2 KiB text, 0 BSS. Existing current SCHC vector test passes 12/12. Recommendation: keep current LICHEN profile implementation for embedded nodes, use libSCHC only as a reference/prototype or possible future gateway-side dependency. project-LICHEN-9nrr.11 should factor a generic Zephyr SCHC module from current code rather than wrapping libSCHC by default.
close_reason: Completed libSCHC evaluation and recorded recommendation in Bead notes plus Beads memory schc-libschc-evaluation-2026-06-30-evaluated-upstream. Evaluated upstream imec-idlab/libschc at a01d7856243d3aa1242918e31649d8a5ee92872e. GPL-3.0 licensing is compatible with LICHEN GPL firmware, but libSCHC is not a Zephyr module, is documented upstream as experimental/missing RFC 8724 features, uses compile-time config/rule headers and platform callbacks, and has a much larger rough embedded footprint than the current profile implementation. Rough arm-zephyr-eabi -Os object measurement: libSCHC example config about 41.9 KiB text and 3.8 KiB BSS before GC; current LICHEN schc.c about 6.2 KiB text and 0 BSS. Existing LICHEN SCHC standalone vectors pass 12/12. Recommendation: do not import/swap libSCHC for embedded nodes now; use it only as reference/prototype or possible future gateway-side dependency. Existing project-LICHEN-9nrr.11 covers the next step: factor a generic Zephyr SCHC module from current code rather than wrapping libSCHC by default. Review pass 1 checked acceptance coverage. Review pass 2 checked source/measurement reproducibility and follow-up coverage; no additional follow-up needed.
